skill.name: creating-skills
skill.description: Guides creation and improvement of Claude Code skills with clear structure, discoverable descriptions, concise examples, and practical quality checks.
skill.summary: Create concise, discoverable, and actionable AI coding skills.
skill.author: AgentWorkforce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown examples, inline backticks, and ordinary prose. No executable helper files, network behavior, credential access, malicious intent, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Markdown backticks, fenced examples, and an illustrative bash snippet as command execution. The file is a written guide and contains no executable script file or instruction to run untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are Markdown code fences and inline examples inside SKILL.md. They are not active code paths and do not execute commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts align with words such as description, describes, and red flags rather than cryptographic functions or algorithms. No hashing, encryption, or random generation code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: A targeted search found no cryptographic API, weak algorithm name, or security-sensitive crypto workflow. The cited lines are skill-writing guidance.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Benign Prose
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts occur in writing guidance and compression examples, including reserved-name advice and example conversation text. They do not collect host, network, user, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation text only. No commands such as host discovery, process listing, credential lookup, or environment enumeration are present.

content.value_statement: Teams need reusable instructions that AI agents can find and follow quickly. This skill guides concise skill structure, discovery wording, examples, checks, and iterative improvement.
actual_capabilities: Defines the required SKILL.md frontmatter and document structure.
Explains how to write searchable descriptions with clear triggers.
Provides guidance for examples, workflows, checklists, and supporting files.
Shows how to keep skills concise for token efficiency.
Includes a quality checklist for reviewing new or revised skills.
limitations: Does not generate or validate files automatically.
Does not include a runnable test harness for skill evaluation.
Focuses on Claude Code skill authoring, with only general guidance for other tools.
Requires human judgment to decide whether a technique deserves a skill.

use_cases.title: Create a New Skill
use_cases.description: Use the structure, naming rules, and checklist to draft a new reusable skill for repeated workflows.

use_cases.title: Improve Skill Discoverability
use_cases.description: Rewrite descriptions with clear triggers, keywords, and expected outcomes so agents select the skill correctly.

use_cases.title: Review Marketplace Submissions
use_cases.description: Check whether a submitted skill is concise, actionable, well scoped, and supported by useful examples.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill for [workflow]. Include frontmatter, a short overview, when to use it, and one practical example.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill for discoverability, clarity, token efficiency, and actionability. Suggest concrete edits and explain each change.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Quality Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a review checklist for a skill about [topic]. Include structure, examples, limitations, validation steps, and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Evaluation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design test scenarios for this skill. Cover correct activation, non-activation, edge cases, and expected outputs for each scenario.

output_examples.input: User asks for help creating a skill for flaky asynchronous tests.
output_examples.output: A concise skill draft with a clear trigger description, focused workflow steps, one practical example, and a review checklist.

output_examples.input: User provides an existing skill that feels too long and hard to scan.
output_examples.output: A revised outline that removes redundant sections, improves headings, tightens examples, and preserves only reusable guidance.

output_examples.input: User wants to know whether a technique should become a skill.
output_examples.output: A decision summary comparing reuse value, project specificity, complexity, and expected future benefit.

best_practices: Write descriptions with both activation triggers and the help the skill provides.
Keep examples concrete, minimal, and close to real work.
Use checklists and validation steps for fragile or high-impact workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not create skills for one-off project conventions.
Do not use vague descriptions such as generic helper labels.
Do not include large reference material in the main skill file.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create and improve Claude Code skills that are discoverable, concise, and actionable.

faq.question: Is this skill only for Claude Code?
faq.answer: It is written for Claude Code skills, but many documentation and example-writing principles apply to Codex and Claude workflows.

faq.question: Does it create files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides authoring guidance, structure, examples, and review criteria for human or agent-assisted editing.

faq.question: When should I avoid creating a skill?
faq.answer: Avoid creating one for one-time fixes, obvious practices, or project-specific conventions that belong in local documentation.

faq.question: How does it improve skill discovery?
faq.answer: It teaches trigger-based descriptions, relevant keywords, clear naming, and focused summaries that agents can match to tasks.

faq.question: What should reviewers check first?
faq.answer: Check the frontmatter description, scope, example quality, token efficiency, and whether the skill solves a reusable problem.
